title: Annual growth of exports of goods and services
subtitle: Exports of goods and services represent the value of all goods and other market services provided to the rest of the world.
caption:
Annual growth of exports of goods and services

Exports of goods and services represent the value of all goods and other market services provided to the rest of the world.

This choropleth world map displays the annual growth of exports of goods and services for the year 2023. The color scale ranges from deep red, representing a contraction of -10%, through yellow at 0%, to dark blue, indicating growth of 10% or higher. Distinct diagonal hatching marks regions with no available data, including Greenland, Russia, and parts of Southeast Asia. The map reveals a varied global landscape of export performance. In Africa, a cluster of nations including Sudan, South Sudan, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo appear in the darkest red shades, placing them in the lowest band near -10%. Conversely, several countries in the Middle East and Central Asia, such as Iran, Iraq, and Kazakhstan, are colored in the darkest blue, signifying growth rates at or above the 10% mark. Across Europe, the picture is mixed; while Finland and parts of Eastern Europe show dark blue high-growth areas, other nations sit in the orange and yellow bands, indicating negative or stagnant growth. In the Americas, Mexico and Argentina are shaded in orange, falling between -5% and -2%, whereas Canada and Australia appear in lighter blue tones, suggesting moderate positive growth in the 2% to 5% range. Large portions of South America and Asia display light blue and yellow hues, clustering in the lower positive or near-zero growth categories. The visual distribution highlights significant regional disparities, with the most severe contractions concentrated in specific African nations and the strongest expansions found in parts of the Middle East and Central Asia.
